seo.title: Joshua Tree Health | Teen PHP & IOP in Apex, NC
seo.description: PHP and IOP mental health care for adolescents ages 10–17 in Apex, NC, grounded in DBT, polyvagal-informed care, and family involvement.

hero.ledeOne: For teens who need more than a weekly appointment.
hero.ledeTwo: Structured PHP and IOP in Apex.

manifesto.title: When weekly therapy isn't enough.
paragraphs: IOP and PHP provide more time, structure, and clinical support without an overnight stay.

Some teens come when symptoms are getting harder to manage between weekly sessions. Others need a steady transition home after inpatient or residential care. We meet both moments with a clear plan and a consistent week.

Care includes DBT skills groups, individual therapy, family involvement, and expressive work through art, music, and mindfulness. Teens return home every evening and practice what they are learning in daily life.

conditions.title: Depression & low mood
conditions.body: Pulling away from people and activities, low energy, sleep that is off, or irritability that will not lift.

conditions.title: Anxiety & panic
conditions.body: Worry that does not switch off, avoidance, panic symptoms, or intense fear around school and social situations.

conditions.title: ADHD & executive functioning
conditions.body: Trouble starting or finishing tasks, acting before thinking, and strong reactions to frustration or perceived failure.

conditions.title: Trauma & PTSD
conditions.body: Feeling constantly on alert, shutting down, or reacting strongly when the present connects to something painful.

conditions.title: Autism & social communication
conditions.body: Sensory overload, hard transitions, masking exhaustion, or anxiety and low mood alongside autism.

conditions.title: Emotional dysregulation & mood
conditions.body: Big reactions that are hard to control, rapid mood shifts, and a long road back to calm after conflict.

programs.title: Two levels of structured outpatient care.
programs.intro: PHP provides a full treatment day. IOP runs part-day, three to four days a week, mornings or afternoons. Both allow teens to return home every evening.

php.title: Full-day treatment during the week.
php.body: Our highest level of outpatient care, for teens who need more structure and support than weekly or after-school therapy.
features: Psychiatric care coordination as clinically indicated

iop.title: Part-day treatment, three to four days a week.
iop.body: A step below PHP for teens who need more than weekly therapy while continuing school and daily routines.

programs.insuranceNote: We'll verify your insurance benefits and walk you through expected costs before care begins. We do not accept Medicaid.

measure.title: Progress should show up in real life.
measure.intro: We check progress from intake through discharge. If your teen is not improving in the ways that matter, the team revisits the plan.

points.title: Know what the plan is for
points.body: After intake, you will know what your teen is working on and why it matters.

points.title: Look beyond the therapy room
points.body: Progress should be visible at school, with friends, and during the hard moments at home.

points.title: Stay part of the work
points.body: The team will talk with you about how care is going and what you can reinforce at home.

process.title: Three steps to a clearer next move.

steps.title: Fill out the form
steps.body: A two-minute form. No medical details. We cover those by phone.

steps.title: We call you
steps.body: A conversation about what is happening and whether this level of care may fit your teen.

steps.title: Start within days
steps.body: A typical start is three to five days from first contact. We recommend the level of care that fits and build the plan together.

location.title: Apex, NC. Easy access from across the Triangle.
location.intro: Serving families across Cary, Holly Springs, Raleigh, Durham, and Chapel Hill.

closing.title: Not sure which program is right?
closing.body: You do not have to decide before you call. A licensed therapist will assess what your teen needs, and our admissions team will explain the options first.
